title: Structural framework of the Chile margin at the Chile ridge collision zone
journal: Proceedings of the Ocean Drilling Program, Initial Reports
abstract: Three multichannel seismic reflection lines collected on board the Conrad as part of the site survey for ODP Leg 141 drilling examine the tectonic and structural framework of the Chile margin in the vicinity of the Chile Triple Junction. The lines image the final phases of spreading-ridge collision with the lower trench slope, and the structure where a spreading ridge has been subducted 2 km beneath the lower trench slope near the Taitao Fracture Zone.

location.description: Chile Triple Junction area (~46°S) near Taitao Fracture Zone; ODP Leg 141 pre-site survey seismic lines

data.name: R/V Conrad ODP 141 Pre-Site Survey Lines 745, 751, 762 — Chile Triple Junction ~46°S
data.description: Three named MCS reflection lines (745, 751, 762) from R/V Conrad site survey for ODP Leg 141 at the Chile Triple Junction; images spreading ridge subduction 2 km beneath trench slope and Taitao Fracture Zone structure. [R/V Conrad RC2901 (Lamont, early 1990s) — no confirmed public data portal found]

_data_note: ODP Initial Reports chapter; open access at ODP TAMU. RC2901 data accessible via MGDS. Named seismic lines 745, 751, 762.

processing.summary: Three multichannel seismic reflection lines from the R/V Conrad ODP Leg 141 site survey (RC2901 cruise, ~1990) were processed to characterize the tectonic and structural framework of the Chile margin at the Chile Triple Junction (~46°S). The profiles image the active spreading ridge collision with the lower trench slope and a subducted ridge segment ~2 km beneath the trench slope near the Taitao Fracture Zone. Standard processing through NMO, stack and time migration.
processing.notes: Initial processing at Lamont-Doherty Geological Observatory; post-stack time migration using DISCO software at USGS facility, Palo Alto, CA (finite-difference algorithm, up to 45° dip).

analysis_notes: Open access ODP publication available at odp.tamu.edu. RC2901 Conrad cruise data accessible via MGDS. Named seismic lines 745, 751, 762. Source: 10-gun array, 3743 in³. Fold: 30 CMP (25m bins). Processing: Lamont-Doherty + DISCO migration at USGS Palo Alto.
